persona.description: A casual browser with low purchase intent who may leave if approached too strongly.

behavior_description: 0-2s: The shopper steps close to the product display. 2-5s: The shopper tries the product, tests a demo function, or checks fit in a natural way. 5-8s: The shopper continues testing while watching the product response or appearance. 8-10s: The shopper finishes the test and keeps considering the item.

behavior_timeline.event: The shopper steps close to the product display.
The shopper tries the product, tests a demo function, or checks fit in a natural way.
The shopper continues testing while watching the product response or appearance.
The shopper finishes the test and keeps considering the item.

kling_prompt_sections.camera: 10-second fixed in-store surveillance-style shot, slightly elevated oblique angle; customer body trajectory, dwell time, hand movement, product display, and counter area visible; face detail is helpful but not required; one primary shopper, natural movement, no subtitles, no voiceover, no brand logos.
kling_prompt_sections.scene: a jewelry counter with bright but natural display lighting; visible product setup: an accessible demo sample on an open presentation tray on top of the counter, outside any glass case; the shopper can naturally touch only this demo sample, while the enclosed display stays in the background.

behavior_timeline.event: The shopper steps close to the product display.
The shopper tries the product, tests a demo function, or checks fit in a natural way.
The shopper continues testing while watching the product response or appearance.
The shopper finishes the test and keeps considering the item.

kling_prompt_sections.negative: no extreme overhead shot, no tiny distant customer, no occluded product area, no extra shoppers, no completed purchase, no salesperson speech, no exaggerated acting, no floating labels, no hidden-category labels, no intervention labels, no scoring text, no UI overlays.

kling_prompt:
Camera: 10-second fixed in-store surveillance-style shot, slightly elevated oblique angle; customer body trajectory, dwell time, hand movement, product display, and counter area visible; face detail is helpful but not required; one primary shopper, natural movement, no subtitles, no voiceover, no brand logos.
Scene: a jewelry counter with bright but natural display lighting; visible product setup: an accessible demo sample on an open presentation tray on top of the counter, outside any glass case; the shopper can naturally touch only this demo sample, while the enclosed display stays in the background.
Customer behavior style: The shopper stays near the display and examines details with visible curiosity.
Behavior timeline:
- 0-2s: The shopper steps close to the product display.
- 2-5s: The shopper tries the product, tests a demo function, or checks fit in a natural way.
- 5-8s: The shopper continues testing while watching the product response or appearance.
- 8-10s: The shopper finishes the test and keeps considering the item.
Negative constraints: no extreme overhead shot, no tiny distant customer, no occluded product area, no extra shoppers, no completed purchase, no salesperson speech, no exaggerated acting, no floating labels, no hidden-category labels, no intervention labels, no scoring text, no UI overlays.
